meta.title: AllyAuditPro — wellness inspection config (standalone MD audit tool)
meta.product: AllyAuditPro — a standalone Medical-Director tool for auditing non-ACHA cash-pay wellness clinics across IV/IM, peptide, and BHRT lines. Its own app. Reads de-identified encounter data exported from AllyOS clinics.
meta.purpose: Defines the wellness inspection for AllyAuditPro: a 4-stage MD audit (Upload → Auto-select by risk → Per-chart AI review → Soft oversight report). Standalone — not a mode of any other product.
meta.source: RenuviaMD AllyOS — iv-module.json (IV risk), BHRT v1.5 engine §14 (BHRT risk), peptide-packs.json (peptide grade/tier). PHI-free config; the audit handles charts under the MD's own authority.

stage1_session.input: the de-identified encounter list — AUTO from AllyOS chairside (Export audit for MD), or manual CSV for paper clinics

md_compliance_checklist: Valid GFE on file for each patient (within 12-month window), provider-signed
Signed standing orders / collaborative protocol current
Emergency cart stocked + response cards (CARD-A / CARD-V / CARD-VC) posted
Adverse-event / FORM-04 protocol established; MD-notification pathway
Provider licenses active; scope-of-practice respected (RN executes under authorization)

stage2_risk_stratification.score_scale: 1–5  ·  5 = mandatory MD review · 4 = high · 3 = moderate · 2 = routine · 1 = low
stage2_risk_stratification.sample_rule: 100% of score-5 (mandatory) charts + highest-risk remaining to the monthly sample size; a MIX across active lines (e.g. 3 IV / 3 Peptides / 3 BHRT).

moderate_3: documentation gap (no monitoring / missing pre or post vitals)

mandatory_5: testosterone non-HSDD or supraphysiologic / hematocrit concern
moderate_3: stable active therapy with recent follow-up
required_docs: Provider CDS note / order + monitoring plan

peptides.status: ready to port from peptide-packs.json (grade→tier) + peptide-interactions.json
mandatory_5: GLP-1 red flag (MTC/MEN2, pancreatitis) not screened
required_docs: Prescription / order + source documentation + monitoring plan

stage3_chart_review.dual_documents: per chart, the two documents above for that line (GFE/Intake + Monitoring for IV; intake+screen + order/monitoring for BHRT/Peptides)
stage3_chart_review.ai_inspection: AllyOS inspect.js — drop the chart, the AI returns ONLY de-identified compliance fields (GFE / consent / pre+post vitals / monitoring / aftercare / AE / suspended / override + risk signals); never patient identifiers
elements_checked: protocol within ceilings / no unauthorized mixing
scope of practice (RN executes under authorization)

stage4_report.type: SOFT wellness MD Oversight Report (NOT the F.S. 400.9935 statutory package)
sections: Clinic Scope (name / period / lines / operating model / record location / MD role)
Monthly Encounter Review (total visits · charts pulled · mandatory-review · charts reviewed · selection logic)
Monthly MD Conclusion (status: Compliant / Compliant-with-corrections / Needs-follow-up / Hold · MD sign-off date · temporary packet-deletion date ~30 days)
stage4_report.output: preview → download PDF → sign in Adobe → send to clinic for the compliance binder

integration_notes.input_auto: AllyOS clinics: Export audit (for MD) = the Stage-1 list; Export charts for MD (by Encounter ID) = the Stage-3 documents.
integration_notes.input_manual: Paper clinics: emailed CSV (Stage 1) + scanned chart packets (Stage 3) — same wizard.
integration_notes.where: AllyAuditPro is its own standalone app — its own clinic-network dashboard, 4-stage audit wizard, and report builder. It is not a mode of any other tool.
integration_notes.phi: AllyAuditPro handles PHI under the Medical Director's own authority (oversight / healthcare operations) — no BAA required for the MD. AllyOS (the clinic product) stays PHI-free.
